name: Rowan Protection
description: The Rowan, also known as the Mountain Ash, holds a prominent place in Celtic mythology and folklore. It is revered as a potent protector against malevolent forces, witchcraft, and negative energies. Its vibrant red berries and sturdy branches are believed to possess inherent magical properties that ward off evil spirits and offer sanctuary. Celts often planted Rowan trees near their homes and sacred spaces to create a protective barrier, ensuring the safety and well-being of their families and communities. The wood of the Rowan was also used to craft protective amulets, wands, and other ritual tools, further enhancing its protective capabilities. In some Celtic traditions, the Rowan is associated with divination and foresight, believed to grant access to hidden knowledge and the ability to perceive future events. The berries, though slightly poisonous, were sometimes consumed in small quantities during rituals to enhance spiritual awareness and connection to the otherworld. The Rowan's resilience in harsh environments also contributed to its symbolism of strength, endurance, and overcoming adversity. Its bright berries, appearing even in late autumn, served as a reminder of hope and the enduring power of life even in the face of darkness. The Rowan is more than just a tree; it's a symbol of resilience, protection, and a connection to the ancient wisdom of the Celtic world. It is also a tree of balance, connecting the physical and spiritual realms, and is often seen as a guardian of sacred spaces and thresholds.
subtitle: Guardian Tree against Dark Magic

keyMyths.title: Rowan and the Witch
keyMyths.description: A tale tells of a witch who attempted to curse a village, but the Rowan trees surrounding it shielded the inhabitants, deflecting her magic. The Rowan's protective power saved the village from harm.
keyMyths.source: Folk Tales of the British Isles

sources.author: Miranda Green
sources.work: Myths and Symbols in Pagan Europe
sources.description: Explores the symbolic significance of trees in Celtic and other European pagan traditions.
sources.text: Explores the symbolic significance of trees in Celtic and other European pagan traditions.
